basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: al-Ḥashr — “The Gathering” — relates the exile of the Banū Naḍīr, those People of the Book who broke their pact with the Prophet and who, believing their fortresses impregnable, demolished their own houses with their own hands. But the sūra transcends the episode to make of it a lesson (iʿtibār) for “those who have eyes.” Qushayrī opens with a basmala in which the whole cosmos proclaims its servitude: the suns, the moons, the stars, the night and the day, every created thing cries of itself: we are His servants, servants of the One who has never ceased to be. He grafts onto it a great meditation on the futility of asking “why?” of the divine command — he who says to his master “why?” does not succeed — and praises the generosity of the Anṣār, models of self-preference (īthār): the altruist prefers to himself the one who is sated while he himself is hungry; the noble builds his house for his guests, the base for himself alone. The sūra ends on one of the Qurʾān's highest constellations of divine Names — the King, the All-Holy, Peace, the Creator, the Fashioner — preceded by the overwhelming verse of the mountain that would split in humility were this Qurʾān sent down upon it.
themes: not asking why of the divine command

versets.ar: سَبَّحَ لِلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ۖ وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: sabbaḥa lillāhi mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi wa-huwa l-ʿazīzu l-ḥakīmu
versets.trad: All that is in the heavens and all that is on the earth glorifies God. He is the Mighty, the Wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: all that He created He made a proof of His oneness, and a road for whoever wishes to know His divinity. He perfected every thing — proof of His knowledge and wisdom; He ordered every thing — witness to His will. And He is al-ʿAzīz: no like equals Him, no associate disputes Him in the Kingdom; al-Ḥakīm: the Judge in whom no flaw is found, upon whom no reproach falls.

mots.racine: س ب ح
mots.sens: glorifies God

mots.ar: مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ
mots.tr: mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍ
mots.racine: س م و / أ ر ض
mots.sens: what is in the heavens and the earth

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ح ك م
mots.sens: the Mighty, the Wise

versets.ar: هُوَ الَّذِي أَخْرَجَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ مِن دِيَارِهِمْ لِأَوَّلِ الْحَشْرِ ۚ مَا ظَنَنتُمْ أَن يَخْرُجُوا ۖ وَظَنُّوا أَنَّهُم مَّانِعَتُهُمْ حُصُونُهُم مِّنَ اللَّهِ فَأَتَاهُمُ اللَّهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَمْ يَحْتَسِبُوا ۖ وَقَذَفَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمُ الرُّعْبَ ۚ يُخْرِبُونَ بُيُوتَهُم بِأَيْدِيهِمْ وَأَيْدِي الْمُؤْمِنِينَ فَاعْتَبِرُوا يَا أُولِي الْأَبْصَارِ
versets.tr: huwa lladhī akhraja lladhīna kafarū min ahli l-kitābi min diyārihim li-awwali l-ḥashri mā ẓanantum an yakhrujū wa-ẓannū annahum māniʿatuhum ḥuṣūnuhum mina llāhi fa-atāhumu llāhu min ḥaythu lam yaḥtasibū wa-qadhafa fī qulūbihimu r-ruʿba yukhribūna buyūtahum bi-aydīhim wa-aydī l-muʾminīna fa-ʿtabirū yā ulī l-abṣāri
versets.trad: It is He who drove out from their homes, at the first gathering, those of the People of the Book who disbelieved. You did not think they would leave, and they thought their fortresses would protect them against God. But God came upon them from where they did not expect, and cast terror into their hearts. They demolished their houses with their own hands and the hands of the believers. Take a lesson from it, O you who have eyes!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse a school of the lesson (iʿtibār): when God wills to subdue an enemy, He makes its lion meek as a she-camel. Several teaching points: “you did not think they would leave” — to preserve the believers from self-satisfaction; “they thought their fortresses impregnable” — yet whoever strengthens himself by a creature, it delivers him to humiliation. And he transposes: they demolish their houses with their hands — and their hearts by following the passions of their soul, and their religion by mixing it with innovations.

mots.ar: أَخْرَجَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ
mots.tr: akhraja lladhīna kafarū min ahli l-kitāb
mots.racine: خ ر ج / ك ف ر / ك ت ب
mots.sens: He drove out the disbelievers of the People of the Book
mots.def: akhraja: to drive out, expel; ahl al-kitāb: People of the Book.

mots.racine: م ن ع / ح ص ن
mots.sens: their fortresses would protect them

mots.racine: ق ذ ف / ر ع ب
mots.sens: He cast terror into their hearts

mots.racine: ع ب ر / ب ص ر
mots.sens: take a lesson, O you who have eyes
mots.def: iʿtabara: to take a lesson; abṣār: sights, eyes.

versets.ar: وَلَوْلَا أَن كَتَبَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمُ الْجَلَاءَ لَعَذَّبَهُمْ فِي الدُّنْيَا ۖ وَلَهُمْ فِي الْآخِرَةِ عَذَابُ النَّارِ
versets.tr: wa-lawlā an kataba llāhu ʿalayhimu l-jalāʾa la-ʿadhdhabahum fī d-dunyā wa-lahum fī l-ākhirati ʿadhābu n-nāri
versets.trad: Had God not decreed exile for them, He would surely have punished them in this world; but in the next world the punishment of the Fire awaits them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: had God not decreed that they would leave, He would have punished them with death and extermination; then, in the Hereafter, the punishment of the Fire awaits them. The exile (jalāʾ) was, in one sense, a veiled mercy: banishment spared them immediate annihilation. God's decree measures even severity.

mots.ar: وَلَوْلَا أَن كَتَبَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمُ الْجَلَاءَ
mots.tr: wa-lawlā an kataba llāhu ʿalayhimu l-jalāʾ
mots.racine: ك ت ب / ج ل و
mots.sens: had God not decreed their exile
mots.def: kataba: to decree, prescribe; jalāʾ: banishment.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب
mots.sens: He would have punished them in this world
mots.def: ʿadhdhaba: to punish; dunyā: this world.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ن و ر
mots.sens: the punishment of the Fire

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ شَاقُّوا اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ ۖ وَمَن يُشَاقِّ اللَّهَ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ شَدِيدُ الْعِقَابِ
versets.tr: dhālika bi-annahum shāqqū llāha wa-rasūlahu wa-man yushāqqi llāha fa-inna llāha shadīdu l-ʿiqābi
versets.trad: That is because they made a breach with God and His Messenger. To whoever makes a breach with Him, God assigns a terrible punishment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī illumines the word shāqqa: mushāqqa is for a man to cross to the other side (shiqq). The sinner who leaves the camp of the obedient for that of the rebels “makes a breach with God.” Disobedience is not merely the breaking of a rule: it is a change of camp, a crossing to the other side of the line.

mots.racine: ش ق ق
mots.sens: they made a breach with God and His Messenger
mots.def: shāqqa: to separate, cross to the other side, oppose.

mots.racine: ش ق ق
mots.sens: whoever makes a breach with God

mots.racine: ش د د / ع ق ب
mots.sens: terrible in punishment

versets.ar: مَا قَطَعْتُم مِّن لِّينَةٍ أَوْ تَرَكْتُمُوهَا قَائِمَةً عَلَىٰ أُصُولِهَا فَبِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ وَلِيُخْزِيَ الْفَاسِقِينَ
versets.tr: mā qaṭaʿtum min līnatin aw taraktumūhā qāʾimatan ʿalā uṣūlihā fa-bi-idhni llāhi wa-li-yukhziya l-fāsiqīna
versets.trad: Whatever palm-trees you cut down or left standing on their roots, it was by God's leave, and that He might disgrace the perverse.
themes: the divine command without a why
versets.commentaire: When the Jews asked “what is the use of cutting down these palms?”, God settled it: it was by His leave. Qushayrī draws a great spiritual rule: the Law is not justified by causes; when the revealed command comes, the quest for the why falls and tongues fall silent. And he warns the disciples: the masters have said: whoever says to his master “why?” does not succeed. Any aspirant in whom such thoughts revolve attains nothing. Whoever does not strip his heart of the search for the cause, who does not settle into fair contentment with all that comes — that one is in nothing of God.

mots.racine: ق ط ع / ل ي ن
mots.sens: whatever palm-trees you cut down
mots.def: qaṭaʿa: to cut; līna: young palm-tree.

mots.racine: ق و م / أ ص ل
mots.sens: standing on their roots

mots.racine: أ ذ ن
mots.sens: by God's leave

mots.racine: خ ز ي / ف س ق
mots.sens: that He might disgrace the perverse
mots.def: akhzā: to put to shame; fāsiq: perverse.

versets.ar: وَمَا أَفَاءَ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ رَسُولِهِ مِنْهُمْ فَمَا أَوْجَفْتُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ خَيْلٍ وَلَا رِكَابٍ وَلَٰكِنَّ اللَّهَ يُسَلِّطُ رُسُلَهُ عَلَىٰ مَن يَشَاءُ ۚ وَاللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-mā afāʾa llāhu ʿalā rasūlihi minhum fa-mā awjaftum ʿalayhi min khaylin wa-lā rikābin wa-lākinna llāha yusalliṭu rusulahu ʿalā man yashāʾu wa-llāhu ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīrun
versets.trad: As for the spoils God granted His Messenger from them, you spurred neither horses nor mounts for it. But God gives His messengers power over whom He wills. God is powerful over all things!
versets.commentaire: The spoils gained without battle (fayʾ) go to the Prophet. Qushayrī recalls the distinction: fayʾ is what reaches the Muslims of the disbelievers' wealth without battle or charging of horses; booty (ghanīma) is what is won by fighting. Victory is not the fruit of the horsemen's effort but of the power God grants to whom He wills: what falls without toil reminds us that every gift comes from Him.

mots.racine: ف ي أ
mots.sens: what God granted His Messenger

mots.ar: فَمَا أَوْجَفْتُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ خَيْلٍ وَلَا رِكَابٍ
mots.tr: fa-mā awjaftum ʿalayhi min khaylin wa-lā rikāb
mots.racine: و ج ف / خ ي ل / ر ك ب
mots.sens: you spurred neither horses nor mounts
mots.def: awjafa: to make run; khayl: horses; rikāb: mounts.

mots.racine: س ل ط / ر س ل
mots.sens: He gives His messengers power over whom He wills

versets.ar: مَّا أَفَاءَ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ رَسُولِهِ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْقُرَىٰ فَلِلَّهِ وَلِلرَّسُولِ وَلِذِي الْقُرْبَىٰ وَالْيَتَامَىٰ وَالْمَسَاكِينِ وَابْنِ السَّبِيلِ كَيْ لَا يَكُونَ دُولَةً بَيْنَ الْأَغْنِيَاءِ مِنكُمْ ۚ وَمَا آتَاكُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَمَا نَهَاكُمْ عَنْهُ فَانتَهُوا ۚ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ ۖ إِنَّ اللَّهَ شَدِيدُ الْعِقَابِ
versets.tr: mā afāʾa llāhu ʿalā rasūlihi min ahli l-qurā fa-lillāhi wa-lir-rasūli wa-li-dhī l-qurbā wa-l-yatāmā wa-l-masākīni wa-bni s-sabīli kay lā yakūna dūlatan bayna l-aghniyāʾi minkum wa-mā ātākumu r-rasūlu fa-khudhūhu wa-mā nahākum ʿanhu fa-ntahū wa-ttaqū llāha inna llāha shadīdu l-ʿiqābi
versets.trad: The spoils God granted His Messenger from the people of the towns belong to God and to the Messenger, and to kin, orphans, the poor, and the wayfarer, so that it not circulate only among the rich of you. Whatever the Messenger gives you, take it; and whatever he forbids you, abstain from it. Fear God! Truly, God is severe in punishment!
versets.commentaire: “Whatever the Messenger gives you, take it; whatever he forbids you, abstain from it”: Qushayrī makes of this a foundation of the obligation to follow him and cling to his way. And he draws from the distribution a spiritual lesson: the heart's liberation from compensations and possessions is the attribute of the masters and the great. Whoever remains captive to his avarice (shuḥḥ) stays in constriction, ceaselessly seeking to collect his shares — that one has nothing of the savours of this Path. The people of purity keep no trace of it.

mots.racine: أ ه ل / ق ر ي
mots.sens: from the people of the towns

mots.ar: كَيْ لَا يَكُونَ دُولَةً بَيْنَ الْأَغْنِيَاءِ
mots.tr: kay lā yakūna dūlatan bayna l-aghniyāʾ
mots.racine: د و ل / غ ن ي
mots.sens: so it not circulate among the rich
mots.def: dūla: what circulates, monopoly; aghniyāʾ: the rich.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / أ خ ذ
mots.sens: what the Messenger gives you, take it
mots.def: ātā: to give; akhadha: to take.

mots.racine: ن ه ي
mots.sens: what he forbids you, abstain from it
mots.def: nahā: to forbid; intahā: to abstain.

versets.ar: لِلْفُقَرَاءِ الْمُهَاجِرِينَ الَّذِينَ أُخْرِجُوا مِن دِيَارِهِمْ وَأَمْوَالِهِمْ يَبْتَغُونَ فَضْلًا مِّنَ اللَّهِ وَرِضْوَانًا وَيَنصُرُونَ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الصَّادِقُونَ
versets.tr: lil-fuqarāʾi l-muhājirīna lladhīna ukhrijū min diyārihim wa-amwālihim yabtaghūna faḍlan mina llāhi wa-riḍwānan wa-yanṣurūna llāha wa-rasūlahu ulāʾika humu ṣ-ṣādiqūna
versets.trad: [The spoils are] for the poor Emigrants who were driven from their homes and their possessions, seeking grace and good pleasure from God, and giving help to God and His Messenger. Those are the truthful!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī defines the truthful poor man (al-faqīr al-ṣādiq): he who abandons every cause and attachment, empties his hours for the worship of God, inclines his heart toward nothing but God, and stands with the Real, content with the running of His decree upon him. Poverty (faqr) is no endured misery but a chosen destitution, in which the heart has no other wealth than its Lord — and it is this that makes truthfulness.

mots.racine: ف ق ر / ه ج ر
mots.sens: for the poor Emigrants

mots.racine: ب غ ي / ف ض ل / ر ض و
mots.sens: seeking grace and good pleasure from God

mots.racine: ص د ق
mots.sens: those are the truthful

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ تَبَوَّءُوا الدَّارَ وَالْإِيمَانَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ يُحِبُّونَ مَنْ هَاجَرَ إِلَيْهِمْ وَلَا يَجِدُونَ فِي صُدُورِهِمْ حَاجَةً مِّمَّا أُوتُوا وَيُؤْثِرُونَ عَلَىٰ أَنفُسِهِمْ وَلَوْ كَانَ بِهِمْ خَصَاصَةٌ ۚ وَمَن يُوقَ شُحَّ نَفْسِهِ فَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna tabawwaʾū d-dāra wa-l-īmāna min qablihim yuḥibbūna man hājara ilayhim wa-lā yajidūna fī ṣudūrihim ḥājatan mimmā ūtū wa-yuʾthirūna ʿalā anfusihim wa-law kāna bihim khaṣāṣatun wa-man yūqa shuḥḥa nafsihi fa-ulāʾika humu l-mufliḥūna
versets.trad: And those who, before them, were settled in the abode and in faith, love those who emigrated to them and find in their breasts no want for what was given them. They prefer others to themselves, even if poverty afflicts them. Whoever is guarded against his own greed — those are the ones who prosper!
versets.commentaire: The great verse of altruism (īthār). Qushayrī makes of it a Sufi anthology: the altruist prefers to himself the one who is sated while he himself is hungry; the noble builds his house for his guests and brothers, the base for himself alone. Whoever distinguishes between one person and another is no man of īthār: one must prefer all, without distinction. Altruism is to see that what is in people's hands is theirs, and that what falls into your hand is but a deposit entrusted to you, awaiting permission to use it. And he notes the divine precision: God did not say “whoever fears the avarice of his soul” (yattaqī) but “whoever is guarded from it” (yūqa) — for the guarding comes from Him, not from oneself.

mots.racine: ب و أ / أ م ن
mots.sens: they settled in the abode and faith
mots.def: tabawwaʾa: to settle, reside; dār: abode (Medina).

mots.racine: أ ث ر
mots.sens: they prefer others to themselves
mots.def: āthara: to prefer, set before oneself (īthār).

mots.racine: خ ص ص
mots.sens: even if they are in need

mots.racine: و ق ي / ش ح ح
mots.sens: whoever is guarded from his soul's avarice

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ جَاءُوا مِن بَعْدِهِمْ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَا اغْفِرْ لَنَا وَلِإِخْوَانِنَا الَّذِينَ سَبَقُونَا بِالْإِيمَانِ وَلَا تَجْعَلْ فِي قُلُوبِنَا غِلًّا لِّلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا رَبَّنَا إِنَّكَ رَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna jāʾū min baʿdihim yaqūlūna rabbanā ghfir lanā wa-li-ikhwāninā lladhīna sabaqūnā bi-l-īmāni wa-lā tajʿal fī qulūbinā ghillan lilladhīna āmanū rabbanā innaka raʾūfun raḥīmun
versets.trad: And those who came after them say: “Our Lord! Forgive us and our brothers who preceded us in faith. Put no rancour in our hearts toward those who believe. Our Lord! You are, in truth, kind, compassionate!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī widens the verse to all generations: those who emigrated after them, then the believers until the Day of Resurrection — all ask mercy for the forerunners, follow the way of compassion toward all Muslims, and beg God to put in their hearts no rancour (ghill) against any believer. And he settles it: whoever has no compassion for the whole body of Muslims has no share in religion. Faith is not solitary: it links each heart to the whole chain of believers.

mots.racine: ج ي أ
mots.sens: they came after them

mots.racine: س ب ق / أ م ن
mots.sens: preceded us in faith

mots.racine: ج ع ل / غ ل ل
mots.sens: put no rancour in our hearts

mots.racine: ر أ ف / ر ح م
mots.sens: You are kind, compassionate

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ نَافَقُوا يَقُولُونَ لِإِخْوَانِهِمُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ لَئِنْ أُخْرِجْتُمْ لَنَخْرُجَنَّ مَعَكُمْ وَلَا نُطِيعُ فِيكُمْ أَحَدًا أَبَدًا وَإِن قُوتِلْتُمْ لَنَنصُرَنَّكُمْ وَاللَّهُ يَشْهَدُ إِنَّهُمْ لَكَاذِبُونَ
versets.tr: a-lam tara ilā lladhīna nāfaqū yaqūlūna li-ikhwānihimu lladhīna kafarū min ahli l-kitābi la-in ukhrijtum la-nakhrujanna maʿakum wa-lā nuṭīʿu fīkum aḥadan abadan wa-in qūtiltum la-nanṣurannakum wa-llāhu yashhadu innahum la-kādhibūna
versets.trad: Do you not see the hypocrites, how they say to their brothers among the People of the Book who disbelieved: “If you are driven out, we will leave with you and never obey anyone against you; if you are attacked, we will help you.” But God bears witness that they are liars!
themes: the hollow promises of the hypocrites
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: these hypocrites of Medina made a pact with the Banū Naḍīr, promising them unwavering support — but God disclosed that they were not what they claimed. They will not help one another; they will betray one another; and if they aid them in some war, they will abandon them the moment they see them fleeing before those they fight. The word of alliance founded on a lie shatters at the first trial.

mots.racine: ن ف ق
mots.sens: those who practise hypocrisy

mots.racine: خ ر ج
mots.sens: if you are driven out, we will leave with you

mots.racine: ق ت ل / ن ص ر
mots.sens: if you are attacked, we will help you
mots.def: qūtila: to be fought; naṣara: to help.

mots.racine: ش ه د / ك ذ ب
mots.sens: God bears witness that they are liars

versets.ar: لَئِنْ أُخْرِجُوا لَا يَخْرُجُونَ مَعَهُمْ وَلَئِن قُوتِلُوا لَا يَنصُرُونَهُمْ وَلَئِن نَّصَرُوهُمْ لَيُوَلُّنَّ الْأَدْبَارَ ثُمَّ لَا يُنصَرُونَ
versets.tr: la-in ukhrijū lā yakhrujūna maʿahum wa-la-in qūtilū lā yanṣurūnahum wa-la-in naṣarūhum la-yuwallunna l-adbāra thumma lā yunṣarūna
versets.trad: If they are driven out, they will not leave with them; if they are attacked, they will not help them; and even if they did help them, they would turn their backs, and then they would not be helped.
versets.commentaire: God unrolls in advance the sequence of perjury: no shared exile, no help, and if help there be, it will be a flight. Qushayrī said it at the prior verse: the hypocrites' alliance has no substance because their hearts are divided. He who acts only by calculation abandons as soon as the calculation turns against him; only fidelity sealed in God holds under fire.

mots.racine: خ ر ج
mots.sens: they will not leave with them

mots.racine: ن ص ر
mots.sens: they will not help them

mots.racine: و ل ي / د ب ر
mots.sens: they will turn their backs
mots.def: wallā l-dubur: to take flight; adbār: backs.

versets.ar: لَأَنتُمْ أَشَدُّ رَهْبَةً فِي صُدُورِهِم مِّنَ اللَّهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَوْمٌ لَّا يَفْقَهُونَ
versets.tr: la-antum ashaddu rahbatan fī ṣudūrihim mina llāhi dhālika bi-annahum qawmun lā yafqahūna
versets.trad: You inspire more dread in their breasts than God Himself, because they are a people who do not understand.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Muslims inspire in their breasts more dread than God — and this because of the weakness of their certainty (yaqīn) and the turning of their hearts away from God. Such is the mark of a heart empty of faith: it trembles before the creature more than before the Creator, because it sees only the apparent causes and not the One who holds them.

mots.racine: ر ه ب
mots.sens: you are more dreaded

mots.racine: ص د ر
mots.sens: in their breasts

mots.racine: ف ق ه
mots.sens: a people who do not understand

versets.ar: لَا يُقَاتِلُونَكُمْ جَمِيعًا إِلَّا فِي قُرًى مُّحَصَّنَةٍ أَوْ مِن وَرَاءِ جُدُرٍ ۚ بَأْسُهُم بَيْنَهُمْ شَدِيدٌ ۚ تَحْسَبُهُمْ جَمِيعًا وَقُلُوبُهُمْ شَتَّىٰ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَوْمٌ لَّا يَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: lā yuqātilūnakum jamīʿan illā fī quran muḥaṣṣanatin aw min warāʾi judurin baʾsuhum baynahum shadīdun taḥsabuhum jamīʿan wa-qulūbuhum shattā dhālika bi-annahum qawmun lā yaʿqilūna
versets.trad: They will not fight you all together except in fortified towns or from behind walls. Their valour among themselves is great! You think them united, but their hearts are divided. That is because they are a people who do not reason.
versets.commentaire: A pivotal verse for Qushayrī: the union of bodies with the discord and divergence of hearts is the root of all corruption, the cause of every abandonment, what emboldens the enemy. The accord of hearts, sharing one aspiration and one intention, engenders every victory and every happiness — and this never occurs among enemies: in them is only the disorder of every state and the rupture of every bond. True unity is not the gathering of bodies but the communion of hearts.

mots.racine: ق ر ي / ح ص ن
mots.sens: in fortified towns

mots.racine: ب أ س / ش د د
mots.sens: their valour among themselves is great

mots.racine: ح س ب / ش ت ت
mots.sens: you think them united, their hearts are scattered

mots.racine: ع ق ل
mots.sens: a people who do not reason

versets.ar: كَمَثَلِ الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ قَرِيبًا ۖ ذَاقُوا وَبَالَ أَمْرِهِمْ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: ka-mathali lladhīna min qablihim qarīban dhāqū wabāla amrihim wa-lahum ʿadhābun alīmun
versets.trad: They are like those shortly before them who tasted the consequences of their conduct, and for them is a painful punishment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reports that the precedent evoked is that of the Banū Qaynuqāʿ or the Naḍīr, whose fall was very recent. The taste (dhāqa) of the dire consequence (wabāl) is served as a warning: what has just struck some lies in wait for others who follow the same way. Recent history is the clearest mirror of the present.

mots.racine: م ث ل / ق ر ب
mots.sens: like those shortly before them
mots.def: mathal: example, like; qarīb: near, recent.

mots.racine: ذ و ق / و ب ل
mots.sens: they tasted the dire outcome of their conduct

versets.ar: كَمَثَلِ الشَّيْطَانِ إِذْ قَالَ لِلْإِنسَانِ اكْفُرْ فَلَمَّا كَفَرَ قَالَ إِنِّي بَرِيءٌ مِّنكَ إِنِّي أَخَافُ اللَّهَ رَبَّ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: ka-mathali sh-shayṭāni idh qāla lil-insāni kfur fa-lammā kafara qāla innī barīʾun minka innī akhāfu llāha rabba l-ʿālamīna
versets.trad: They are like Satan when he says to man: “Disbelieve!” Then when he disbelieves, he says: “I disown you! I fear God, the Lord of the worlds.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī widens the image: so it is with the people of lapse, the men of fault, the pretentious — all on one level in this matter: their companionship in God does not profit. He cites: “Close friends, that Day, will be enemies to one another, save the godfearing.” The tempter seduces then abandons; every alliance not sealed in the fear of God turns into disavowal. Today each pairs with his like: the pretender to the pretender, the man of meaning to the man of meaning.

mots.racine: ق و ل / ك ف ر
mots.sens: when he says to man: disbelieve

mots.racine: ب ر أ
mots.sens: I disown you

mots.racine: خ و ف / ر ب ب / ع ل م
mots.sens: I fear God, the Lord of the worlds

versets.ar: فَكَانَ عَاقِبَتَهُمَا أَنَّهُمَا فِي النَّارِ خَالِدَيْنِ فِيهَا ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ جَزَاءُ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-kāna ʿāqibatahumā annahumā fī n-nāri khālidayni fīhā wa-dhālika jazāʾu ẓ-ẓālimīna
versets.trad: So the end of both is that they are in the Fire, abiding therein forever. Such is the recompense of the wrongdoers.
versets.commentaire: The tempter and the tempted end together in the Fire: the alliance of evil saves none of its accomplices. The end (ʿāqiba) reveals the truth of a companionship — and he who bound himself outside of God finds himself bound to his partner in loss. Such is the recompense (jazāʾ) of the unjust: to reap together what they sowed together.

mots.racine: ع ق ب
mots.sens: the end of both was

mots.racine: خ ل د
mots.sens: abiding therein forever

mots.racine: ج ز ي / ظ ل م
mots.sens: the recompense of the wrongdoers

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَلْتَنظُرْ نَفْسٌ مَّا قَدَّمَتْ لِغَدٍ ۖ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ خَبِيرٌ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū ttaqū llāha wa-l-tanẓur nafsun mā qaddamat li-ghadin wa-ttaqū llāha inna llāha khabīrun bimā taʿmalūna
versets.trad: O you who believe! Fear God, and let every soul consider what it has sent ahead for tomorrow! Fear God! Truly, God is fully aware of what you do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes the two “Fear God”: the first taqwā bears on the remembrance of punishment and reflection on the deed, its good and its evil; the second is the taqwā of vigilance (murāqaba) and self-reckoning (muḥāsaba) — whoever holds no reckoning of his acts nor vigilance over his states will soon be exposed. He ranks men: one muses on his yesterday — what was allotted to him?; another on his tomorrow — what will he meet?; the third holds to his present moment, fulfilling what it requires, absorbed in his Remembered One, watching neither past nor future.

mots.racine: و ق ي
mots.sens: fear God

mots.racine: ن ظ ر / ق د م
mots.sens: let the soul consider what it sent ahead for tomorrow
mots.def: naẓara: to consider; qaddama: to send ahead; ghad: tomorrow.

mots.racine: خ ب ر / ع م ل
mots.sens: aware of what you do

versets.ar: وَلَا تَكُونُوا كَالَّذِينَ نَسُوا اللَّهَ فَأَنسَاهُمْ أَنفُسَهُمْ ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْفَاسِقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lā takūnū ka-lladhīna nasū llāha fa-ansāhum anfusahum ulāʾika humu l-fāsiqūna
versets.trad: Do not be like those who forgot God, so He made them forget themselves. Those are the perverse.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the root of all evil is the forgetting of the Lord; without forgetting, there would be no disobedience. He who forgets God, God makes him forget himself — that is, forget the care of his own soul: he no longer strives to realize his repentance, and he defers the obedience the moment imposes on him. To forget God is to lose oneself: for the soul finds itself again only by turning toward its Origin.

mots.racine: ن س ي
mots.sens: they forgot God

mots.racine: ن س ي / ن ف س
mots.sens: He made them forget themselves
mots.def: ansā: to make forget; anfus: souls.

mots.racine: ف س ق
mots.sens: those are the perverse

versets.ar: لَا يَسْتَوِي أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ وَأَصْحَابُ الْجَنَّةِ ۚ أَصْحَابُ الْجَنَّةِ هُمُ الْفَائِزُونَ
versets.tr: lā yastawī aṣḥābu n-nāri wa-aṣḥābu l-jannati aṣḥābu l-jannati humu l-fāʾizūna
versets.trad: The inhabitants of the Fire and the inhabitants of the Garden are not equal. The inhabitants of the Garden, they are the triumphant!
themes: the inequality of the two abodes
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī transposes: the people of heedlessness (ghafla) are not equal to the people of union (waṣla). The difference is not only of abode but of the heart's state: he who lived in forgetfulness (verse 19) and he who lived in remembrance cannot reach the same end. Triumph (fawz) belongs to those who did not forget.

mots.racine: س و ي
mots.sens: are not equal

mots.racine: ص ح ب / ن و ر / ج ن ن
mots.sens: the inhabitants of the Fire and the Garden

mots.racine: ف و ز
mots.sens: they are the triumphant
mots.def: fāʾiz: one who triumphs, the winner.

versets.ar: لَوْ أَنزَلْنَا هَٰذَا الْقُرْآنَ عَلَىٰ جَبَلٍ لَّرَأَيْتَهُ خَاشِعًا مُّتَصَدِّعًا مِّنْ خَشْيَةِ اللَّهِ ۚ وَتِلْكَ الْأَمْثَالُ نَضْرِبُهَا لِلنَّاسِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: law anzalnā hādhā l-qurʾāna ʿalā jabalin la-raʾaytahu khāshiʿan mutaṣaddiʿan min khashyati llāhi wa-tilka l-amthālu naḍribuhā lin-nāsi laʿallahum yatafakkarūna
versets.trad: Had We sent down this Qurʾān upon a mountain, you would have seen it humbled and split apart from the fear of God. Such are the parables We set forth for people, that they may reflect.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī offers two readings. The first: if the mountain had reason and heart, and this Qurʾān were sent down upon it, it would submit and break in humility — an image of the hardness of human hearts before the Qurʾān. The second, higher: this is no reproach but praise and the mark of a distinction by strength: “had We sent down this Qurʾān upon a mountain,” it could not have borne it and would have split — yet these I have distinguished by that strength, until they could bear the weight of My speech.

mots.ar: لَوْ أَنزَلْنَا هَٰذَا الْقُرْآنَ عَلَىٰ جَبَلٍ
mots.tr: law anzalnā hādhā l-qurʾāna ʿalā jabal
mots.racine: ن ز ل / ج ب ل
mots.sens: had We sent down this Qurʾān upon a mountain
mots.def: anzala: to send down; jabal: mountain.

mots.racine: خ ش ع / ص د ع
mots.sens: humbled and splitting
mots.def: khāshiʿ: humble, submissive; mutaṣaddiʿ: splitting apart.

mots.racine: خ ش ي
mots.sens: from the reverential fear of God

mots.racine: ف ك ر
mots.sens: that they may reflect

versets.ar: هُوَ اللَّهُ الَّذِي لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ عَالِمُ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهَادَةِ ۖ هُوَ الرَّحْمَٰنُ الرَّحِيمُ
versets.tr: huwa llāhu lladhī lā ilāha illā huwa ʿālimu l-ghaybi wa-sh-shahādati huwa r-raḥmānu r-raḥīmu
versets.trad: He is God — there is no god but He! He is the Knower of the hidden and the manifest. He is the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
themes: the knowledge of the hidden and the seen
versets.commentaire: The first of the three great stations of Names that seal the sūra. Qushayrī: the hidden (ghayb) is what is known neither by necessity nor by inference — what the Real has reserved to Himself, opening the way to it for no one; the manifest (shahāda) is what creatures know. In sum: no known thing escapes His knowledge. And He joins to His knowledge two Names of tenderness — al-Raḥmān, al-Raḥīm — for the One who knows all is also the One who forgives all.

mots.racine: أ ل ه
mots.sens: no deity but He

mots.racine: ع ل م / غ ي ب / ش ه د
mots.sens: Knower of the hidden and the manifest

mots.racine: ر ح م
mots.sens: the All-Merciful, the Compassionate
mots.def: raḥmān: universal mercy; raḥīm: particular mercy.

versets.ar: هُوَ اللَّهُ الَّذِي لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْمَلِكُ الْقُدُّوسُ السَّلَامُ الْمُؤْمِنُ الْمُهَيْمِنُ الْعَزِيزُ الْجَبَّارُ الْمُتَكَبِّرُ ۚ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ
versets.tr: huwa llāhu lladhī lā ilāha illā huwa l-maliku l-quddūsu s-salāmu l-muʾminu l-muhayminu l-ʿazīzu l-jabbāru l-mutakabbiru subḥāna llāhi ʿammā yushrikūna
versets.trad: He is God — there is no god but He, the King, the All-Holy, the Peace, the Faithful, the Vigilant, the Mighty, the Compeller, the Supreme. Glory be to God, far above what they associate!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds each Name: al-Malik, the King, who has power to bring into being; al-Quddūs, the All-Holy, free of all defect; al-Salām, Peace, free of all imperfection, who greets His saints and from whose punishment the believers are kept safe; al-Muʾmin, who attests His servant's truthfulness in his tawḥīd and says to him: you have spoken truly, My servant; al-Muhaymin, the Witness, the Faithful Guardian; al-ʿAzīz, the Victorious whom none overcomes, without peer; al-Jabbār, whom hands do not reach, who mends what is broken; al-Mutakabbir, the All-Holy above all failing. A cascade of glory culminating in tasbīḥ.

mots.racine: م ل ك / ق د س / س ل م
mots.sens: the King, the All-Holy, the Peace
mots.def: malik: king; quddūs: all-holy; salām: peace.

mots.racine: أ م ن / ه م ن
mots.sens: the Faithful, the Vigilant
mots.def: muʾmin: who gives security; muhaymin: guardian, witness.

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ج ب ر / ك ب ر
mots.sens: the Mighty, the Compeller, the Supreme
mots.def: ʿazīz: mighty; jabbār: who compels and mends; mutakabbir: majestic.

mots.racine: س ب ح / ش ر ك
mots.sens: glory to God above what they associate
mots.def: subḥāna: glory, transcendence; ashraka: to associate.

versets.ar: هُوَ اللَّهُ الْخَالِقُ الْبَارِئُ الْمُصَوِّرُ ۖ لَهُ الْأَسْمَاءُ الْحُسْنَىٰ ۚ يُسَبِّحُ لَهُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۖ وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: huwa llāhu l-khāliqu l-bāriʾu l-muṣawwiru lahu l-asmāʾu l-ḥusnā yusabbiḥu lahu mā fī s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-huwa l-ʿazīzu l-ḥakīmu
versets.trad: He is God — the Creator, the Originator, the Fashioner! To Him belong the most beautiful Names. All that is in the heavens and the earth glorifies Him. He is the Mighty, the Wise.
versets.commentaire: The triad of creation: al-Khāliq (who decrees being), al-Bāriʾ (who brings forth from nothing), al-Muṣawwir (who gives form). Qushayrī: He is the Producer of essences and traces; to Him the most beautiful Names — the excellent named ones. The sūra closes as it opened, on the universal glorification (yusabbiḥu lahu mā fī s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍ): from the fall of the proud to the most beautiful Names, all reality is but one single song of glory.

mots.racine: خ ل ق / ب ر أ / ص و ر
mots.sens: the Creator, the Originator, the Fashioner
mots.def: khāliq: creator; bāriʾ: who brings forth; muṣawwir: who fashions.

mots.racine: س م و / ح س ن
mots.sens: to Him the most beautiful Names
mots.def: asmāʾ ḥusnā: the most beautiful Names.

mots.ar: يُسَبِّحُ لَهُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ
mots.tr: yusabbiḥu lahu mā fī s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍ
mots.racine: س ب ح
mots.sens: all in the heavens and earth glorifies Him

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ح ك م
mots.sens: the Mighty, the Wise
